introduction: This spec defines the `akc admin` CLI command group — a Cobra-based command tree that wraps Go SDK admin-only client methods for user administration, organization administration, and credential (key/token) management, delegating all API communication to the Go SDK (spec 12) and using shared CLI infrastructure (spec 13). Note: requirements referencing apikit SDK APIs (spec 12) and CLI core helpers (spec 13) are based on PRD-stated interfaces and should be cross-checked against the installed packages before implementation.

glossary.AddOrgMember: SDK method on `apikit.Client` that adds a user to an organization (returns only error; HTTP 204).
glossary.BlockOrg: SDK method on `apikit.Client` that blocks an organization.
glossary.BlockUser: SDK method on `apikit.Client` that blocks a user.
glossary.CreateOrg: SDK method on `apikit.Client` that creates a new organization.
glossary.CreateUser: SDK method on `apikit.Client` that creates a new user.
glossary.DeleteOrg: SDK method on `apikit.Client` that deletes an organization (returns only error; HTTP 204).
glossary.DemoteUser: SDK method on `apikit.Client` that revokes the admin role from a user.
glossary.GetUserByID: SDK method on `apikit.Client` that retrieves a single user by ID.
glossary.IncludeBlocked: Boolean field in `apikit.ListUsersOptions` and `apikit.ListOrgsOptions` that controls whether blocked resources appear in list responses.
glossary.ListOrgMembers: SDK method on `apikit.Client` that lists members of an organization.
glossary.ListOrgs: SDK method on `apikit.Client` that lists organizations with optional filtering.
glossary.ListUserKeys: SDK method on `apikit.Client` that lists a user's API key metadata.
glossary.ListUserTokens: SDK method on `apikit.Client` that lists a user's personal access token metadata.
glossary.ListUsers: SDK method on `apikit.Client` that lists users with optional filtering options.
glossary.NewAdminCmd: The exported Go function that returns the root `*cobra.Command` for the admin command tree; called by CLI core (spec 13) to register admin subcommands on the root.
glossary.PromoteUser: SDK method on `apikit.Client` that grants the admin role to a user.
glossary.RemoveOrgMember: SDK method on `apikit.Client` that removes a user from an organization (returns only error; HTTP 204).
glossary.RevokeUserKey: SDK method on `apikit.Client` that revokes a user's API key (returns only error; HTTP 204).
glossary.RevokeUserToken: SDK method on `apikit.Client` that revokes a user's personal access token (returns only error; HTTP 204).
glossary.UnblockOrg: SDK method on `apikit.Client` that unblocks an organization.
glossary.UnblockUser: SDK method on `apikit.Client` that unblocks a user.
glossary.UpdateOrg: SDK method on `apikit.Client` that updates an organization's fields.
glossary.UpdateUserByID: SDK method on `apikit.Client` that updates a user's fields by ID.
glossary.admin bootstrap: The process described in spec 05 that generates the break-glass admin token used for initial administrative access.
glossary.admin_bootstrap: Spec 05 that generates the break-glass admin token.
glossary.agent interface: The structured JSON output of `akc help --json` describing all commands with their arguments, flags, HTTP method/path, and auth requirements; format defined in spec 13.
glossary.akc: The CLI binary for apikit administration.
glossary.akc admin: The top-level CLI command group requiring admin-level credentials.
glossary.akc help --json: The agent interface command that emits a structured JSON description of all registered commands, their arguments, flags, HTTP mappings, and auth requirements.
glossary.apikit.APIError: Go SDK error type (spec 12) representing an API-returned HTTP error with a numeric code and message.
glossary.apikit.APIKeyMeta: Go SDK type (spec 12) representing API key metadata (never includes secret key material).
glossary.apikit.Client: The Go SDK client type (spec 12) that exposes all admin API methods used by the CLI.
glossary.apikit.CreateOrgRequest: Go SDK request struct (spec 12) for `CreateOrg`, containing `Name`, `Slug`, and `URL` (pointer) fields.
glossary.apikit.CreateUserRequest: Go SDK request struct (spec 12) for `CreateUser`, containing `Username`, `Email`, `Provider`, and `ProviderID` fields.
glossary.apikit.ListOrgsOptions: Go SDK options struct (spec 12) for `ListOrgs`, containing the `IncludeBlocked` boolean field.
glossary.apikit.ListUsersOptions: Go SDK options struct (spec 12) for `ListUsers`, containing the `IncludeBlocked` boolean field.
glossary.apikit.Organization: Go SDK type (spec 12) representing an organization resource; the JSON shape printed by org commands.
glossary.apikit.PAT: Go SDK type (spec 12) representing personal access token metadata (never includes secret token material).
glossary.apikit.UpdateOrgRequest: Go SDK request struct (spec 12) for `UpdateOrg`, containing `Name` and `URL` as `*string` pointer fields (nil means do not update).
glossary.apikit.UpdateUserRequest: Go SDK request struct (spec 12) for `UpdateUserByID`, containing a plain `string` field `FullName`.
glossary.apikit.User: Go SDK type (spec 12) representing a user resource; the JSON shape printed by user commands.
glossary.break-glass admin token: A special admin token generated by the admin bootstrap process (spec 05) that grants administrative access for recovery scenarios.
glossary.cobra.ExactArgs: Cobra validation function that enforces an exact number of positional arguments, rejecting commands with too few or too many.
glossary.context.Background: The Go standard library context with no deadline or cancellation, used for all SDK calls in the CLI.
glossary.empty patch: A PATCH call where no fields are modified, e.g. `akc admin orgs update` invoked with neither `--name` nor `--url`; the CLI warns on stderr via `warnf` and still calls the SDK.
glossary.error envelope: The JSON error format `{"error": {"code": N, "message": "..."}}` emitted to stdout for all error conditions.
glossary.handleError: CLI core helper (spec 13) that formats an error as a JSON error envelope to stdout and returns the appropriate exit code (1 for API errors, 2 for client errors).
glossary.include-blocked: CLI flag (`--include-blocked`) that causes list commands to include blocked resources in the response.
glossary.internal/cli: The Go package directory containing all CLI implementation code for the apikit admin commands.
glossary.loadConfig: CLI core helper (spec 13) that loads endpoint URL, user ID, and API key from config file, environment variables, and command-line flags in precedence order.
glossary.newClient: CLI core helper (spec 13) that constructs an `apikit.Client` from a loaded `Config`.
glossary.pass-through validation: The policy of forwarding flag and argument values (including empty strings and non-UUID IDs) to the SDK and server without client-side format validation.
glossary.printJSON: CLI core helper (spec 13) that serializes a value as indented JSON to stdout using `json.MarshalIndent` with two-space indentation.
glossary.test-scoped interface: An unexported Go interface defined in a `_test.go` file used only for injecting mock SDK clients in unit tests, not part of the production API surface.
glossary.void response: A command whose underlying SDK method returns only `error` (HTTP 204); the CLI prints `{}` to stdout on success.
glossary.warnf: CLI core helper (spec 13) that writes a formatted warning message to stderr with a consistent prefix and newline convention defined in spec 13.
glossary.*cobra.Command: The Cobra library's command type; each CLI subcommand is represented as a *cobra.Command.
glossary.Run: The Cobra command field that holds the function executed when the command is invoked.
glossary.RunE: The Cobra command field that holds the function executed when the command is invoked, returning an error.
glossary.cobra.NoArgs: Cobra validation function that rejects any positional arguments.
glossary.context.Background(): Go standard library call that returns a non-nil, empty context with no deadline or cancellation.
glossary.json.MarshalIndent: Go standard library function that serializes a value as indented JSON.
glossary.net/http: Go standard library package for HTTP client and server implementations.
glossary.*apikit.APIError: Pointer to the Go SDK error type representing an API-returned HTTP error.
glossary.[]*apikit.User: Go slice of pointers to User objects, returned by list and member endpoints.
glossary.[]*apikit.Organization: Go slice of pointers to Organization objects, returned by list endpoints.
glossary.[]*apikit.APIKeyMeta: Go slice of pointers to APIKeyMeta objects, returned by key listing endpoints.
glossary.[]*apikit.PAT: Go slice of pointers to PAT objects, returned by token listing endpoints.
glossary.Response[User].Data: The typed data field on the Response wrapper, containing the unwrapped User object.
glossary.nil: Go's zero value for pointer types, slices, maps, interfaces, and channels.
glossary.false: Go boolean literal representing the false value.
glossary.{}: Empty JSON object printed to stdout for void-response commands (HTTP 204).
glossary.urlPtr: A *string pointer variable holding the --url flag value, nil when the flag is not provided.
glossary.URL: The optional URL field on Organization and related request structs.
glossary.FullName: \"\": An UpdateUserRequest with FullName set to empty string, which clears the user's full name.
glossary.internal/cli/admin*.go: Glob pattern matching all admin command production source files in the internal/cli package.
glossary.users: Cobra subcommand name for the admin users command group.
glossary.orgs: Cobra subcommand name for the admin orgs command group.
glossary.keys: Cobra subcommand name for the admin keys command group.
glossary.tokens: Cobra subcommand name for the admin tokens command group.
glossary.members: Cobra subcommand name for the admin orgs members command group.
glossary.list: Cobra subcommand name for listing resources.
glossary.show: Cobra subcommand name for showing a single resource.
glossary.create: Cobra subcommand name for creating a resource.
glossary.update: Cobra subcommand name for updating a resource.
glossary.delete: Cobra subcommand name for deleting a resource.
glossary.promote: Cobra subcommand name for granting the admin role to a user.
glossary.demote: Cobra subcommand name for revoking the admin role from a user.
glossary.block: Cobra subcommand name for blocking a resource.
glossary.unblock: Cobra subcommand name for unblocking a resource.
glossary.add: Cobra subcommand name for adding a member to an organization.
glossary.remove: Cobra subcommand name for removing a member from an organization.
glossary.revoke: Cobra subcommand name for revoking a credential.
glossary.\"admin\": The Cobra Use string for the admin parent command.
glossary.admin users: The admin users command group parent.
glossary.admin keys: The admin keys command group parent.
glossary.admin tokens: The admin tokens command group parent.
glossary.<id>: Positional argument placeholder for a resource UUID (user ID or org ID).
glossary.<user_id>: Positional argument placeholder for a user UUID.
glossary.<org_id>: Positional argument placeholder for an organization UUID.
glossary.<key_id>: Positional argument placeholder for an API key identifier.
glossary.<token_id>: Positional argument placeholder for a PAT identifier.
glossary.<arg_name>: Placeholder for the descriptive name of a positional argument in error messages.
glossary.id: Positional argument name representing a resource UUID in CLI commands.
glossary.user_id: Positional argument name representing a user UUID in admin key and token commands.
glossary.key_id: Positional argument name representing an API key identifier.
glossary.org_id: Positional argument name representing an organization UUID.
glossary.token_id: Positional argument name representing a PAT identifier.
glossary.--include-blocked: Boolean flag that includes blocked resources in list command output.
glossary.--username: Required string flag for specifying a username in user creation.
glossary.--email: Required string flag for specifying an email in user creation.
glossary.--provider: Required string flag for specifying an OAuth provider in user creation.
glossary.--provider-id: Required string flag for specifying a provider-specific user ID in user creation.
glossary.--full-name: Required string flag for specifying a user's full name in update commands.
glossary.--name: String flag for specifying an organization name.
glossary.--slug: Required string flag for specifying an organization slug.
glossary.--url: Optional string flag for specifying an organization URL.
glossary.--username \"\": An explicitly empty --username flag value, passed through to the SDK without client-side validation.
glossary.--full-name \"\": An explicitly empty --full-name flag value, which is valid and clears the user's full name.
glossary.name: The name field in agent interface command metadata entries.
glossary.description: The description field in agent interface command metadata entries.
glossary.method: The HTTP method field in agent interface command metadata entries.
glossary.path: The API path field in agent interface command metadata entries.
glossary.args: The positional arguments array in agent interface command metadata entries.
glossary.flags: The flags array in agent interface command metadata entries.
glossary.code: 0: Error envelope code value indicating a client-side error (not an HTTP status).
glossary.method: \"GET\": Agent interface metadata indicating the command maps to an HTTP GET request.
glossary.method: \"POST\": Agent interface metadata indicating the command maps to an HTTP POST request.
glossary.method: \"PATCH\": Agent interface metadata indicating the command maps to an HTTP PATCH request.
glossary.method: \"PUT\": Agent interface metadata indicating the command maps to an HTTP PUT request.
glossary.method: \"DELETE\": Agent interface metadata indicating the command maps to an HTTP DELETE request.
glossary.auth: \"admin\": Agent interface metadata indicating the command requires admin-level authentication.
glossary.path: \"/users\": Agent interface API path for user listing.
glossary.path: \"/users/:id\": Agent interface API path for single user operations.
glossary.path: \"/users/:id/promote\": Agent interface API path for user promotion.
glossary.path: \"/users/:id/demote\": Agent interface API path for user demotion.
glossary.path: \"/users/:id/block\": Agent interface API path for user blocking.
glossary.path: \"/users/:id/unblock\": Agent interface API path for user unblocking.
glossary.path: \"/users/:id/keys\": Agent interface API path for listing a user's API keys.
glossary.path: \"/users/:id/keys/:key_id\": Agent interface API path for revoking a user's API key.
glossary.path: \"/users/:id/tokens\": Agent interface API path for listing a user's PATs.
glossary.path: \"/users/:id/tokens/:token_id\": Agent interface API path for revoking a user's PAT.
glossary.path: \"/orgs\": Agent interface API path for organization listing.
glossary.path: \"/orgs/:id\": Agent interface API path for single organization operations.
glossary.path: \"/orgs/:id/block\": Agent interface API path for organization blocking.
glossary.path: \"/orgs/:id/unblock\": Agent interface API path for organization unblocking.
glossary.path: \"/orgs/:id/members\": Agent interface API path for listing organization members.
glossary.path: \"/orgs/:id/members/:user_id\": Agent interface API path for adding/removing organization members.
glossary.\"admin users list\": The agent interface name for the admin users list command.

glossary.akc admin keys revoke: CLI command to revoke a user's API key (admin).
glossary.akc admin orgs delete: CLI command to delete an organization (admin).
glossary.akc admin orgs members add: CLI command to add a member to an organization (admin).
glossary.akc admin orgs members remove: CLI command to remove a member from an organization (admin).
glossary.akc admin tokens revoke: CLI command to revoke a user's PAT (admin).
glossary.\"auth\": \"admin\": Agent interface metadata field indicating the command requires admin-level authentication.

user_story.goal: invoke `akc admin` subcommands that are organized in a nested Cobra command tree
user_story.benefit: navigate and discover all admin operations without needing documentation

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a `*cobra.Command` with Use set to `"admin"` that has no `Run` function and registers the `users`, `orgs`, `keys`, and `tokens` subcommand groups as children
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: the returned command tree satisfies `cmd.HasSubCommands() == true` and `cmd.RunE == nil`

acceptance_criteria.action: registers subcommands: `list`, `show`, `create`, `update`, `promote`, `demote`, `block`, `unblock` — each as a direct child of the `admin users` parent command
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: all eight subcommands are discoverable via `akc admin users --help`

acceptance_criteria.action: registers subcommands: `list`, `create`, `update`, `delete`, `block`, `unblock`, and a `members` sub-group with `list`, `add`, `remove` — all as children in the correct hierarchy
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: all org subcommands are discoverable via `akc admin orgs --help` and member subcommands via `akc admin orgs members --help`

acceptance_criteria.action: registers subcommands: `list` and `revoke` as children of the `admin keys` parent command
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: both subcommands are discoverable via `akc admin keys --help`

acceptance_criteria.action: registers subcommands: `list` and `revoke` as children of the `admin tokens` parent command
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: both subcommands are discoverable via `akc admin tokens --help`

acceptance_criteria.action: is registered on the Cobra root command exclusively by CLI core (spec 13); the admin package does not self-register on the root command
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: the `internal/cli` package exports only `NewAdminCmd` for registration; all other constructor functions are unexported

edge_cases.system: any parent command in the admin tree (`admin`, `admin users`, `admin orgs`, `admin orgs members`, `admin keys`, `admin tokens`)
edge_cases.action: prints the help text for that command group to stdout and exits with code 0; no SDK call is made and no `loadConfig` is invoked
edge_cases.return_contract: exit code 0; help text printed to stdout

user_story.goal: have every admin command follow a consistent, predictable execution sequence
user_story.benefit: rely on uniform behavior for configuration loading, SDK invocation, output formatting, and error handling across all admin commands

acceptance_criteria.system: every admin subcommand with a `Run` function
acceptance_criteria.action: executes in the following order: (1) calls `loadConfig` to obtain endpoint URL and API key, (2) calls `newClient` to construct an `apikit.Client`, (3) parses positional arguments and flags, (4) validates required arguments and flags, (5) calls the corresponding SDK method with `context.Background()`, (6) on success calls `printJSON` with the SDK response and exits 0
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: stdout contains valid JSON; exit code is 0 on success

acceptance_criteria.action: calls `handleError` with the error, prints the JSON error envelope `{"error": {"code": <http_status>, "message": "<server_message>"}}` to stdout, and exits with code 1
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 1; stdout contains `{"error": {"code": N, "message": "..."}}`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the SDK method returns a value of type `*apikit.APIError`

acceptance_criteria.action: calls `handleError` with the error, prints a JSON error envelope with `code: 0` to stdout, and exits with code 2
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 2; stdout contains `{"error": {"code": 0, "message": "..."}}`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the SDK method returns a non-`*apikit.APIError` error (e.g. network failure, context cancellation)

acceptance_criteria.action: prints a JSON error envelope with `code: 0` to stdout via `handleError` and exits with code 2; no SDK call is made
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 2; stdout contains `{"error": {"code": 0, "message": "..."}}`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `loadConfig` returns an error (missing endpoint URL or API key)

acceptance_criteria.action: uses `context.Background()` as the context argument for all SDK method calls; no deadlines or timeouts are set on the context
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: SDK method receives a non-nil context with no deadline

acceptance_criteria.action: never makes direct HTTP calls; all API communication is delegated exclusively to `apikit.Client` methods
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: no `net/http` client is constructed or used directly in `internal/cli/admin*.go` production files

edge_cases.action: exits with code 2; the failure is treated as a client error
edge_cases.error_condition: `printJSON` fails because stdout is closed or serialization fails

edge_cases.action: Cobra terminates the process; no special cleanup is required because `context.Background()` has no cancellation and the CLI is a short-lived process; partial stdout output may be incomplete
edge_cases.return_contract: process is terminated by the signal; exit code is signal-determined
edge_cases.error_condition: an OS signal (SIGINT/SIGTERM) is received while an SDK call is in progress

user_story.goal: receive a machine-parseable JSON error on stdout with exit code 2 when required arguments or flags are missing
user_story.benefit: distinguish client-side input errors (exit 2) from server-side rejections (exit 1) without parsing error message text

acceptance_criteria.system: any admin command that requires positional arguments
acceptance_criteria.action: prints `{"error": {"code": 0, "message": "missing required argument: <arg_name>"}}` to stdout and exits with code 2; no SDK call is made; `<arg_name>` is the descriptive name from the command signature (e.g. `id`, `user_id`, `key_id`, `org_id`, `token_id`)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 2; stdout contains the JSON error envelope with code 0
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a required positional argument is absent

acceptance_criteria.system: any admin command that defines required flags
acceptance_criteria.action: prints `{"error": {"code": 0, "message": "missing required flag: --<flag_name>"}}` to stdout and exits with code 2; no SDK call is made
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 2; stdout contains the JSON error envelope with code 0
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a required flag is absent from the command invocation

acceptance_criteria.action: uses `cobra.ExactArgs(N)` (or `cobra.NoArgs` where N=0) to enforce the correct number of positional arguments; Cobra rejects extra arguments before the command `RunE` is called
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: commands invoked with too many positional arguments exit with a Cobra usage error before any SDK call

acceptance_criteria.action: does not validate UUID format or any other format for positional ID arguments; any non-empty string is passed through to the SDK via `pass-through validation`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: non-UUID string IDs are forwarded to the SDK; server-returned 4xx errors are converted to exit code 1

acceptance_criteria.system: every admin command with required string flags
acceptance_criteria.action: does not reject an empty string value for a required flag when the flag is explicitly provided (e.g. `--username ""`); the empty string is passed through to the SDK via `pass-through validation`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: empty-string flag values reach the SDK; server-returned 4xx errors result in exit code 1

user_story.goal: list all users, optionally including blocked ones, via `akc admin users list`
user_story.benefit: audit user accounts and their states without writing custom API clients

acceptance_criteria.action: calls `ListUsers(ctx, &apikit.ListUsersOptions{IncludeBlocked: false})` and prints the returned `[]*apikit.User` as JSON to stdout via `printJSON`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 0; stdout contains a JSON array of user objects

acceptance_criteria.action: calls `ListUsers(ctx, &apikit.ListUsersOptions{IncludeBlocked: true})` and prints the returned `[]*apikit.User` as JSON to stdout
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 0; stdout contains a JSON array including blocked user objects

acceptance_criteria.action: registers in the `agent interface` with `method: "GET"`, `path: "/users"`, `auth: "admin"`, no positional args, and one boolean flag `--include-blocked` with default `false`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `akc help --json` includes an entry for `admin users list` with the specified metadata

user_story.goal: retrieve a single user by ID via `akc admin users show <id>`
user_story.benefit: inspect a specific user's attributes without listing all users

acceptance_criteria.action: calls `GetUserByID(ctx, id)`, unwraps the `Response[User].Data` field, and prints the `apikit.User` as JSON to stdout via `printJSON`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 0; stdout contains a JSON user object
acceptance_criteria.trigger: invoked with a non-empty positional `<id>` argument

acceptance_criteria.action: registers in the `agent interface` with `method: "GET"`, `path: "/users/:id"`, `auth: "admin"`, and one positional argument `id`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `akc help --json` includes an entry for `admin users show` with the specified metadata

user_story.goal: receive all stdout output as valid, consistently formatted JSON
user_story.benefit: parse command output reliably without special-casing different output formats

acceptance_criteria.action: writes to stdout exactly the JSON produced by `printJSON`, which uses `json.MarshalIndent` with two-space indentation; stdout contains valid JSON in all cases (resource object, resource array, or `{}`)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: stdout is always valid JSON; no non-JSON text is written to stdout by the admin commands

acceptance_criteria.system: every admin command that wraps a `void response` SDK method
acceptance_criteria.action: prints the literal empty JSON object `{}` to stdout on success via `printJSON`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: stdout contains `{}` (with optional whitespace per `json.MarshalIndent`)

acceptance_criteria.action: writes human-readable warning and informational messages exclusively to stderr via `warnf`; no such messages appear on stdout
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: stdout contains only JSON; stderr may contain plain-text warnings

user_story.goal: receive structured JSON error envelopes on stdout with a predictable exit code mapping
user_story.benefit: differentiate API errors from client configuration or input errors in scripts without parsing message text

acceptance_criteria.action: on success exits with code 0; on `*apikit.APIError` exits with code 1; on any client error (missing config, invalid args, network failure) exits with code 2
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code is exactly 0, 1, or 2 as specified; no other exit codes are used

acceptance_criteria.action: produces a JSON error envelope on stdout in the form `{"error": {"code": N, "message": "..."}}` where N is the HTTP status for API errors and 0 for client errors
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: stdout contains a valid JSON error envelope; no non-JSON error text appears on stdout

acceptance_criteria.action: prints `{"error": {"code": 403, "message": "forbidden"}}` to stdout via `handleError` and exits with code 1
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 1

acceptance_criteria.action: prints `{"error": {"code": 404, "message": "<server_message>"}}` to stdout via `handleError` and exits with code 1
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 1

user_story.goal: query `akc help --json` to discover all admin commands with their HTTP method, path, auth level, args, and flags
user_story.benefit: dynamically construct valid CLI invocations without hardcoding command signatures

acceptance_criteria.action: registers `agent interface` metadata with `auth: "admin"` plus the correct `method`, `path`, `args`, and `flags` values as specified per-command in this spec; the registration mechanism is defined in spec 13
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `akc help --json` output includes an entry for every admin command with `"auth": "admin"`

acceptance_criteria.system: the `agent interface` entry for every admin command
acceptance_criteria.action: includes the `name` field set to the full command path (e.g. `"admin users list"`), a one-line `description`, `method`, `path`, `args` array with positional argument descriptors, and `flags` array with name, type, required boolean, default value, and description for each flag
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: all specified fields are present and non-empty in the `akc help --json` output entry for each command

requirements.title: Unit test coverage for admin commands

user_story.goal: have comprehensive unit tests for all admin commands using test-scoped mock SDK interfaces
user_story.benefit: verify command behavior in isolation from the network and real server without integration test infrastructure

acceptance_criteria.action: defines a `test-scoped interface` (unexported) covering only the SDK methods exercised by that file's commands, and injects a mock implementation into the command under test
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: tests compile and pass with `go test ./internal/cli/...`; the mock interface is not exported or referenced in production code

acceptance_criteria.action: verifies: (1) the correct SDK method is called with the correct parameters, (2) the JSON output on stdout matches the expected structure, (3) the exit code matches the success/error scenario, and (4) flag and argument validation produces the correct error envelope and exit code 2
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: all named test cases in the Testing Strategy section of this spec pass under `go test ./internal/cli/...`

acceptance_criteria.system: integration tests against a live or stubbed HTTP server
acceptance_criteria.action: are explicitly out of scope for this component; unit tests with mocks are sufficient and are the only required test type
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: no integration test files are required in this spec's repository layout

correctness_properties.title: Stdout is always valid JSON
correctness_properties.invariant: stdout contains exactly one valid JSON value — either a resource object, a resource array, an empty object `{}`, or a JSON error envelope; no non-JSON text is written to stdout

correctness_properties.title: Exit code is always 0, 1, or 2
correctness_properties.invariant: the process exits with code exactly 0 (success), 1 (`*apikit.APIError`), or 2 (client error); no other exit codes are produced

correctness_properties.title: No direct HTTP calls in admin commands
correctness_properties.invariant: no `net/http` client is constructed or used directly; all API communication is delegated exclusively to `apikit.Client` methods

correctness_properties.title: SDK is never called when validation fails
correctness_properties.for_any: admin command invocation where a required positional argument or required flag is absent
correctness_properties.invariant: no SDK method is called; the command exits with code 2 and a JSON error envelope before reaching the SDK call site

correctness_properties.title: Void-response commands always print exactly `{}`
correctness_properties.for_any: invocation of `akc admin orgs delete`, `akc admin orgs members add`, `akc admin orgs members remove`, `akc admin keys revoke`, or `akc admin tokens revoke` that succeeds (SDK returns nil error)
correctness_properties.invariant: stdout contains `{}` and exit code is 0

correctness_properties.title: All admin commands registered with auth: admin in agent interface
correctness_properties.invariant: the `agent interface` entry for the command includes `"auth": "admin"` and the correct `method`, `path`, `args`, and `flags` metadata

correctness_properties.title: `context.Background()` used for all SDK calls
correctness_properties.for_any: SDK method invocation in any admin command
correctness_properties.invariant: the context argument is `context.Background()` — a non-nil context with no deadline or cancellation

execution_paths.title: Successful admin users list (default, no blocked)

steps.action: runs `akc admin users list` in a terminal with a valid config file containing endpoint URL and API key
reads endpoint URL and API key from config and returns a `Config` struct
constructs an `apikit.Client` from the `Config`
calls `ListUsers(context.Background(), &apikit.ListUsersOptions{IncludeBlocked: false})`
makes the HTTP GET /users request and returns `[]*apikit.User`
serializes the `[]*apikit.User` slice as indented JSON and writes it to stdout
exits with code 0

execution_paths.title: API error from admin users show (404 not found)

steps.action: runs `akc admin users show abc-nonexistent-id`
successfully returns a `Config`
constructs an `apikit.Client`
calls `GetUserByID(context.Background(), "abc-nonexistent-id")`
receives HTTP 404 from the server and returns an `*apikit.APIError` with code 404
formats `{"error": {"code": 404, "message": "user not found"}}` and writes it to stdout
exits with code 1

execution_paths.title: Client error — missing required flag for admin users create

steps.action: runs `akc admin users create --username alice --email alice@example.com --provider local` (missing `--provider-id`)
detects that `--provider-id` flag is absent and prints `{"error": {"code": 0, "message": "missing required flag: --provider-id"}}` to stdout
exits with code 2; no SDK call is made
runs `akc admin orgs delete org-uuid-123`
returns a valid `Config`
constructs an `apikit.Client`
calls `DeleteOrg(context.Background(), "org-uuid-123")`
makes HTTP DELETE /orgs/org-uuid-123 and receives HTTP 204; returns nil error
writes `{}` to stdout
exits with code 0

execution_paths.title: Empty patch warning — admin orgs update with no flags

steps.action: runs `akc admin orgs update org-uuid-456` with neither `--name` nor `--url`
returns a valid `Config`
constructs an `apikit.Client`
detects neither flag is provided and calls `warnf("no fields specified for update")` to write a warning to stderr
proceeds to call `UpdateOrg(context.Background(), "org-uuid-456", &apikit.UpdateOrgRequest{Name: nil, URL: nil})`
makes HTTP PATCH /orgs/org-uuid-456 and returns an `apikit.Organization` (HTTP 200)
writes the organization JSON to stdout
exits with code 0

execution_paths.title: Network failure during admin keys list

steps.action: runs `akc admin keys list user-uuid-789`
returns a valid `Config`
constructs an `apikit.Client`
calls `ListUserKeys(context.Background(), "user-uuid-789")`
encounters a TCP connection failure and returns a plain (non-`*apikit.APIError`) error
formats `{"error": {"code": 0, "message": "<network error description>"}}` and writes it to stdout
exits with code 2

execution_paths.title: Full end-to-end flow — agent discovers command via help JSON then invokes admin users create

steps.action: runs `akc help --json` to discover available admin commands and their signatures
returns structured JSON listing all registered commands including `admin users create` with `method: POST`, `path: /users`, `auth: admin`, and all four required flags
constructs and runs `akc admin users create --username bot --email bot@ci.example.com --provider local --provider-id bot-001`
reads endpoint URL and API key from environment variables (per spec 13 precedence rules)
constructs an `apikit.Client` pointing at the configured endpoint
validates all four flags are present and calls `CreateUser(context.Background(), &apikit.CreateUserRequest{Username: "bot", Email: "bot@ci.example.com", Provider: "local", ProviderID: "bot-001"})`
makes HTTP POST /users, receives HTTP 201, and returns `*apikit.User`
serializes the `apikit.User` as indented JSON and writes it to stdout
parses the JSON from stdout and extracts the new user's ID for downstream pipeline steps
exits with code 0

error_handling.condition: Required positional argument is absent from the command invocation
error_handling.behavior: Prints `{"error": {"code": 0, "message": "missing required argument: <arg_name>"}}` to stdout via `handleError` and exits with code 2; no SDK call is made

error_handling.condition: Required flag is absent from the command invocation
error_handling.behavior: Prints `{"error": {"code": 0, "message": "missing required flag: --<flag_name>"}}` to stdout and exits with code 2; no SDK call is made

error_handling.condition: `loadConfig` returns an error (missing endpoint URL, missing API key, or unreadable config file)
error_handling.behavior: Prints a JSON error envelope with `code: 0` to stdout via `handleError` and exits with code 2; no `newClient` or SDK call is made

error_handling.condition: SDK method returns `*apikit.APIError` (server returned a 4xx or 5xx HTTP status)
error_handling.behavior: Calls `handleError` with the `*apikit.APIError`; prints `{"error": {"code": <http_status>, "message": "<server_message>"}}` to stdout and exits with code 1

error_handling.condition: SDK method returns a non-`*apikit.APIError` error (e.g. network failure, DNS resolution failure, connection timeout)
error_handling.behavior: Calls `handleError` with the error; prints `{"error": {"code": 0, "message": "<error_description>"}}` to stdout and exits with code 2

error_handling.condition: Server returns HTTP 403 (caller lacks admin role or uses an invalid API key)
error_handling.behavior: SDK returns `*apikit.APIError` with code 403; CLI prints `{"error": {"code": 403, "message": "forbidden"}}` to stdout and exits with code 1

error_handling.condition: Server returns HTTP 404 (resource not found, including malformed/non-existent IDs due to pass-through validation)
error_handling.behavior: SDK returns `*apikit.APIError` with code 404; CLI prints `{"error": {"code": 404, "message": "<server_message>"}}` to stdout and exits with code 1

error_handling.condition: Server returns HTTP 409 conflict when demoting the last admin
error_handling.behavior: SDK returns `*apikit.APIError` with code 409 and message `"cannot demote the last admin"`; CLI prints the error envelope to stdout and exits with code 1

error_handling.condition: `printJSON` fails (e.g. stdout is closed or the write syscall fails)
error_handling.behavior: Command exits with code 2; the failure is treated as a client error

error_handling.condition: Too many positional arguments are provided to a command
error_handling.behavior: Cobra's `ExactArgs(N)` (or `NoArgs`) validator rejects the invocation before the `RunE` function is called; Cobra prints a usage error; no SDK call is made

symbols.name: Command
symbols.signature: type Command struct { Use string; Short string; Long string; Args PositionalArgs; RunE func(cmd *Command, args []string) error; ... }
symbols.notes: Root struct for all CLI commands; used to build the nested admin command tree.

symbols.name: ExactArgs
symbols.notes: Validates that the command receives exactly n positional arguments; used on all admin subcommands with positional args.

symbols.name: NoArgs
symbols.notes: Validates that the command receives no positional arguments; used on admin commands with no positional args (e.g. `admin users list`, `admin users create`).
